name: Digital Identity Guidelines — Authentication and Lifecycle Management
owner: National Institute of Standards and Technology
description: Authenticator assurance levels (AAL1/2/3), authenticator types, and lifecycle management. The most heavily cited part of the 800-63 series. Companion to 800-63A (Identity Proofing) and 800-63C (Federation).
relationToCloudSecurity: Federal digital-identity assurance baseline for cloud-hosted federal information systems and federated cloud services.
relationToAISecurity: Applies to AI systems handling identity proofing, authentication, or federation within scope.

license.notes: Work of NIST is in the US public domain (17 USC §105).

lifecycle.notes: SP 800-63-3 series remains the most widely-cited federal digital-identity baseline through transition to 800-63-4 (2024).

current_extraction.notes: Source PDF marker-extracted to markdown. extract_800_63b.py walks the numbered-section structure into per-section structured CSV/JSON across 11 chapters (Purpose, Introduction, Definitions, Authenticator Assurance Levels, Authenticator and Verifier Requirements, Authenticator Lifecycle Management, Session Management, Threats, Privacy, Usability, References).
